extraction.one_line_summary: IMPEY Daryl won at 37 after surviving the final second-category climb, first World Tour win since 2019 Tour de France.
tactical_patterns: EF Education used full team domestiques Carthy/Urán on final climb to position Bettiol, revealing deep climbing sprint leadout strategy.
Race leader Williams distanced on final Cat-2 climb but successfully bridged over top, exposing vulnerability on sustained gradients.
Three-man break with Holmes collecting intermediate sprint points strategically killed on final climb by sprinter teams, not before summit.

rider_signals.WILLIAMS Stephen: negative: Distanced on final second-category climb before recovering over top, suggesting fragile form for stage race leader.
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: negative: Led out sprint but faded early despite full team support, questioning form after previous stage win.
rider_signals.IMPEY Daryl: positive: First World Tour win since 2019 TDF stage 9, survived sustained Cat-2 climb at 37, signals unexpected climbing durability.
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: neutral: Working as domestique for Bettiol on final climb contradicts typical GC leader role, suggests EF stage-hunting priority.
rider_signals.KRON Andreas: positive: White jersey holder attempted bonus-second attack that would have taken race lead, showing aggressive GC tactics.
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: negative: DNS after previous day crash, reducing Quick-Step options for stage hunting.
